engine.ladder_reasoning: Not selling defined expertise (no trust), no portfolio-as-proof, and no goods to browse; the visitor is checking a recurring live event/venue — what it is, when/where (Bibliothèque Bruegel, dated shows), and whether to attend — which is the place decision.

goal.one_action: reserve a free seat for an upcoming storytelling show

structure.archetype_note: Static Zola blog/collective site: homepage blends narrative posts + an upcoming-shows table; About covers origin + membership; a third page details the SEPT À NEUF show series. Contact is an off-domain Tally link, not a page.

mapped.observed_as: Les SEPT À NEUF de 7àDire (announcement post)
Pourquoi SEPT À NEUF ? (show format explained)
photo gallery (logo, group, posters, seniors show)

unmapped.observed: Prochains spectacles — dated list of 3 upcoming shows (Oct 28, Nov 25, Jan 20, 7PM) each with a reserve link
unmapped.why_no_fit: No canonical section expresses a dated event schedule / upcoming-shows table; it is neither results, pricing, nor a generic list.

proposed_new.name: event-listings
proposed_new.evidence: Prochains spectacles renders a dated schedule of upcoming shows, each with a reserve link handing off to an external Tally RSVP.

confidence.why: fetch_quality=full; site content and intent are clear. Only caveat: RSVP/Contact forms are off-domain Tally embeds so field-level detail is unseen — does not affect classification.

gap_findings: No canonical section for a dated event schedule / upcoming-shows list (mapped to event-listings capability, unmapped as a section).
Goal is RSVP-to-attend a FREE cultural event with an optional 'panier libre' donation basket — sits between donate-RSVP and form; the taxonomy has no clean 'attend a free event' conversion.
The €10/year membership fee is not a saleable offer/pricing tier — pricing section is a poor fit for a nonprofit membership note.
